source_module: apps/native/lib/formatters.ts (flattened to /app/lib/formatters.ts in the isolated bun workspace)
runtime: bun 1.3.14 (Debian trixie base, git + python3 installed at build time)
gate_revision_note: First gate: Sonnet 0/5 (good), but Opus 0/10 with a diagnostic profile — all 10 trials failed exactly the SAME 2 tests (formatPercentage value>0->=0 zero-sign, formatRelativeTime days>7->=7 week-edge) while solving the other 3 defects 10/10. Those 2 were convention-picks: whether 0% carries a '+' and whether exactly-7-days reads 'Nd ago' vs a date are pure DISPLAY CONVENTIONS with no code-derivable correct side, so they were unsolvable (and since all defects must be found, they sank the whole task). Both were swapped for sibling-pinned mediums (isValidPhone >=10, formatCryptoAmount <0.01), and their gold tests were DELETED (not reclassified to p2p). The 3 that solved 10/10 (formatCompactNumber, formatAddress, isValidEmail) were kept.
f2p: formatters boundary behavior > compact number promotes exactly one thousand to K
formatters boundary behavior > address exactly at the abbreviation floor is left intact
formatters boundary behavior > email with trailing content after the domain is rejected
formatters boundary behavior > phone at exactly the canonical ten-digit length is accepted
formatters boundary behavior > crypto amount exactly on the small-value boundary keeps four decimals
p2p: formatters boundary behavior > compact number formats a mid-range thousands value
formatters boundary behavior > compact number formats a millions value
formatters boundary behavior > compact number formats a billions value
formatters boundary behavior > compact number leaves sub-thousand values unscaled
formatters boundary behavior > address well above the floor is abbreviated at both ends
formatters boundary behavior > address well below the floor is returned unchanged
formatters boundary behavior > a well-formed email is accepted
formatters boundary behavior > a string missing the at-sign is rejected
formatters boundary behavior > a spaced international phone well over ten digits is accepted
formatters boundary behavior > a short numeric string well under ten digits is rejected
formatters boundary behavior > a three-digit string is rejected as a phone
formatters boundary behavior > crypto amount well below the small cutoff uses maximum precision
formatters boundary behavior > crypto amount in the sub-unit band uses four decimals
formatters boundary behavior > crypto amount at or above one unit uses minimum decimals
partial_detail: Fixing 2 of 5 defects (formatCompactNumber + isValidPhone) yields 16/19 required passed; the 3 unfixed f2p still fail -> reward 0.
alt_impl_detail: Materially different but correct fixes (Math.abs(value) >= 1e3; address.length <= chars*2 + 3; a reordered but fully-anchored /^[^@\s]+@[^@\s]+\.[^@\s]+$/; digit-count > 9; value < 0.01) all yield reward 1 -> the gold tests assert behavior, not the oracle tokens.
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN (9 passed / 0 failed in test/formatters.test.ts at the planted state, run offline in-image)
litmus_result: PASS — the instruction names only the module (lib/formatters.ts) and the boundary/edge CLASS of problem with two non-exhaustive illustrative examples; it does not name the functions, the boundary directions, the trigger values, or the defect count. A dev finds the file immediately (allowed) but must audit each helper's boundary logic to find all five slips, with no failing-test gradient to guide them.
derivability_note: Every defect's correct side is pinned by a CONCRETE visible-code artifact, not a display convention: #1 two sibling cutoffs use >=; #2 the parallel sibling band boundary `value < 1` is exclusive; #3 abbreviating at length 2*chars yields a no-shorter string; #4 the leading ^ remains while $ is dropped; #5 the sibling formatPhoneNumber formats exactly cleaned.length === 10 numbers, establishing ten as the canonical valid phone length in the same file. This is the same KIND of sibling/structural pin that the first-gate Opus solved 10/10 on the three kept defects.
harness_notes: bun's default console reporter prints ONLY failing tests, so per-test PASSED verdicts are taken from the JUnit XML (--reporter=junit --reporter-outfile).

parser.py is regex-only (no xml.etree/expat dependency) so it runs on the minimal Debian python3 in the bun image; a <testcase> with a <failure>/<error> child is 'failed', <skipped> is 'skipped', else 'passed'; identity is '<classname/describe> > <name>'.
The image installs git + python3 at build time (oven/bun:1 ships neither); test execution is fully offline (verified with --network none for every ladder run).

defects.derivability: The sibling M and B cutoffs both use '>='; the lone K cutoff using '>' is internally inconsistent, and 1000 is definitionally one thousand.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Visible tests use 999 / 1500 / 2.5M / 3B; none lands on exactly 1000, where '>' and '>=' diverge.

defects.derivability: The parallel sibling band boundary 'else if (value < 1)' is exclusive; the descending precision ladder must use exclusive '<' at both edges, so 0.01 belongs to the next band down (4 decimals).
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Visible tests use 0.00001234 / 0.125 / 12.5; none lands on exactly 0.01, where '<' and '<=' diverge.

defects.edge_input: an address exactly 2*chars long (e.g. '0x123456' with chars=4)
defects.wrong_output: abbreviates into a middle-dotted string no shorter than the original instead of returning it whole
defects.derivability: At length 2*chars the abbreviated form (first-chars + '...' + last-chars) is as long as or longer than the input, so abbreviating is pointless; the guard exists precisely to return such inputs whole.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Visible tests use lengths 5 and 18 with chars=4; neither is exactly 8, the chars*2 boundary.

defects.edge_input: a well-formed address followed by trailing text, e.g. 'user@example.com not-real'
defects.wrong_output: returns true (accepts the prefix) instead of false
defects.derivability: The leading '^' anchor remains while the trailing '$' is gone; anchoring only one end is internally inconsistent for a validator meant to match the whole string.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Visible tests use a clean address (true) and an at-sign-less string (false); neither has trailing junk after a valid prefix, where the missing '$' bites.

defects.wrong_output: returns false (rejects a canonical ten-digit phone) instead of true
defects.derivability: The sibling formatPhoneNumber formats exactly 'cleaned.length === 10' numbers as US phones ((XXX) XXX-XXXX), establishing ten as THE canonical valid phone length in the same file; a validator that rejects ten-digit numbers directly contradicts the formatter that canonically formats them.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Visible tests use an 11-digit international number (true) and a 5-digit string (false); neither is exactly 10, where '>=' and '>' diverge.

fairness_audit: No mocks or hidden coupling: all five targets are pure, exported functions the agent can read, and every gold assertion checks output values, not implementation. The alternative-implementation audit confirms materially different correct fixes (Math.abs()+'>=', an ellipsis-aware length guard, a reordered fully-anchored regex, a digit-count '> 9', and 'value < 0.01') all score reward 1, so no test encodes the oracle's token choice. Every defect's correct side is derivable from a concrete visible-code pin (sibling comparison or sibling function), not a display convention — the two convention-picks that a first gate proved unsolvable were removed. The gold file enters only via config.json test_patch (absent from the image; git history length 1, working tree clean), and the visible suite is green at the planted state, so there is no git or failing-test leak.
